title: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Kgatleng District Council (food-vending) Bye-laws
title_en: Local Government : Subsidiary Legislation : Kgatleng District Council (food-vending) Bye-laws

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
These Bye-laws may be cited as the Kgatleng District Council (Food-Vending) Bye-laws*.
*Originally made under the Township Act now repealed, these regulations have been continued under s 94(2) of the Local Government Act, 2013.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Qualification for carrying on food-vending business

definitions.definition: any utensil, machinery, instrument, apparatus, crockery, article or part thereof, used or intended for use in processing, preparation, handling and serving of food
any person authorised by the Council Secretary in writing for purposes of giving effect to these Bye-laws
the area within the jurisdiction of the Council

glasses, plates, dishes, cups, saucers, spoons, forks, ladles, chopsticks, and any disposable articles used or intended for use in or for the making, preparing, keeping, selling, supplying or the serving of food
any appliances, containers, gargets, implements, crockery, utensils or wrappers intended for a single use in the storage, preparation, display, sale or serving of food
a person who prepares, packs, stores, displays and sells ready-to-eat-food or beverages either from a stall, business premises or from place to place
a person who assists in the preparation, cooking, handling and the serving of food
a person registered as a medical practitioner in terms of section 9 of the Botswana Health Professions Act (Cap. 61:02), entitling him to practice as such in Botswana
any macroscopic living organisms that can cause disease or food spoilage
a food-vendor who moves from place to place for the purpose of processing, preparing, delivering, displaying and serving of ready to eat food

food that consist wholly or partly of milk, milk products, eggs, meat, poultry, fish or other ingredients capable of supporting progressive growth of micro-organisms that cause food spoilage, food-poisoning and food borne diseases
any animals or insect, domestic or otherwise, capable of directly or indirectly contaminating food, and shall include rodents, roaches, flies or other insects or vermin infestation

any food (including beverages other than alcoholic beverages) which is normally consumed in its raw state, or any food handled, processed, mixed, cooked or otherwise prepared into a form in which it is normally consumed without further processing
a cart, caravan or motor-vehicle with or without wheels, or any fixed place equipped with chairs, tables, benches, baskets, or any other structure approved by the Council for food-vending business
appliances, containers, gargets and equipment, including traditional ones, used in the processing, preparation, handling and serving of food
sullage water arising as a result of the activities of food-vending business.
